text:
"Your product, self-evolving" sounds like vaporware.

So here's the boring, real version — the loop that's actually running:

1. A user sends feedback through one embedded widget (no login wall). It lands with the page, the user, and repro context attached.
2. RunHQ turns it into an agent-ready task and spawns your coding agents IN PARALLEL — not one at a time.
3. Each run comes back as a clean GitHub PR: diff, summary, provenance. A human reviews and approves it. Always.
4. The PR gets its own live preview environment — exercise the change end-to-end before it merges.
5. It ships through your existing CD.

Same feedback volume, one team: 5 shipped → 19 shipped once the agents ran in parallel and review/ship stopped being the bottleneck.

Not "AI ships your code while you sleep." You still approve every PR. We just drained everything around the agent that was slowing you down.
first_comment: The loop in 60 seconds, and you can start free: https://runhq.io — happy to answer anything about how the review step works.
media_hint: Bar chart: 5 (sequential) vs 19 (parallel agents), same feedback volume — OR a real PR screenshot with preview-env URL

text:
[RESHARE of the 09:00 page post to personal feed — amplification, not a second post] The hardest part of "AI writes your code now" isn't the writing. It's everything downstream.

The agent finishes in minutes, then the PR sits for hours because reviewing it is the slow part now. Parallelizing the agents only helped once we fixed the review + ship + preview loop around them.

Wrote up the actual loop below 👇 The 5→19 number is the part I didn't believe until we measured it.
